event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a vibrant green-to-blue gradient background displaying 'YOLTZIN HANDMADE' and 'SCRAPBOOK, CARDMAKING & PAPERCRAFTS', accompanied by upbeat, light background music. Galea greets viewers, introducing herself and announcing a showcase of purchases from her favorite store, Acción. The scene transitions to a static, top-down view of a brightly lit, cluttered crafting table, densely packed with scrapbooking supplies from 'Action Bélgica', including colorful washi tapes, clear stamp sets with floral and leaf designs, bright buttons, a blue embossing folder, containers of rhinestones and embellishments, and patterned paper packs. Galea explains her visit to Acción was to find papers for a friend and because she saw on social media that crafters from the Netherlands had purchased new items like die-cuts and embellishments. She expresses disappointment at not finding a specific die-cut blog but hopes to return soon, admiring the items seen in other videos. A hand then enters from the bottom left, gently adjusting the embossing folder and a clear stamp sheet, reinforcing the narration about new products from 'Action' stores arriving first in the Netherlands before Belgium. Galea expresses excitement about finding new stamps and novelties, explaining she was selective to avoid overspending, focusing on summer-themed items and gifts, and reiterates her continuous collection of new stamps due to their quality. The background reveals a green cutting mat and additional crafting items under bright, even lighting.

Scrapbooking Supplies.description: A diverse collection of crafting materials, including washi tapes, colorful buttons, an embossing folder, rhinestones, and prominently, clear stamp sets with floral and leaf designs. The female speaker's narration highlights these items as 'new stamps' and 'novelties' she found, expressing her keen interest in collecting them, especially the stamps, which she considers 'good' and buys whenever new ones arrive.

event_blocks.summary: A female voice begins in Spanish, announcing "Ahora sí, empiezo" (Now I start) as a well-lit display of scrapbooking supplies, including washi tapes, buttons, and patterned paper, is presented. She explains she notes prices to recall them, clarifying "cuánto me costó, perdón" (how much it cost me, sorry), and expresses a preference for economical, functional, and good quality items, particularly "productos de la Action" (Action products). A hand enters, adjusting an embossing folder and a clear stamp sheet before picking up and returning a washi tape package. The speaker then states, "Y comienzo con, bueno, comienzo con las washi tips" (And I start with, well, I start with the washi tapes), noting recent purchases due to limited previous selections. The hand displays three washi tape packages—floral, fruit-themed, and geometric—confirming "tres sets" (three sets) cost "80 centavos cada set" (80 cents each set). The fruit-themed set is described as "muy veraniego" (very summery) with pineapple, watermelon, and lemon motifs. Later, the hand showcases two bags of colorful buttons, labeled 'Buttons' with quantities '52x' and '152x', with the speaker confirming she purchased "estos estos cuatro colores" (these these four colors) for "90 centavos" (90 cents) per bag, despite looking for yellow ones and noting the absence of black, gray, and white. The hand then picks up a package labeled '4 FIGURE ERASERS', revealing four pineapple-shaped erasers in green, pink, yellow, and teal, which cost 40 cents for the set and are described as "cute" and "super pretty," intended for "pretty summer mail" or as gifts. Text overlays like 'Action Bélgica' and 'YOLTZIN HANDMADE' are subtly present throughout the organized, brightly lit crafting environment.

All are labeled 'WASHI TAPE' with dimensions '3x3M - 15MM' (confirmed by the speaker as "3 metros cada washi") and marked with a price tag of '80¢' (confirmed by the speaker as "80 centavos"). The speaker finds them "super lindo" and expresses a preference for the yellow floral one.

event_blocks.summary: Hands hold a clear plastic package labeled 'PINS AND IRON-ON PATCHES' against a backdrop of colorful scrapbooking supplies. A female voice explains in Spanish that the items are generally "para poner en la ropa" (for putting on clothes), though she personally won't use them that way. The package, featuring a light green geometric pattern, contains five embroidered patches: two cactus designs, a llama, a red flower, and a sloth, with the speaker unsure about one specific design, and also "cuatro pins" (four pins). The hands rotate the package, showing details, and point to specific patches like the sloth, cactus, and llama. The speaker reveals she bought two sets at "12 euros" each. The hands then switch to a second package with a pink geometric background, also labeled 'PINS AND IRON-ON PATCHES,' described as "de unicornio así esas cosas" (of unicorn and those things). This package includes designs like stars, a unicorn, a rainbow (referred to as a "nubecita" or little cloud), a heart, an envelope with a heart, and a blue diamond. The hands point to individual patches, highlighting the unicorn, rainbow, and star designs, with the speaker noting one patch "tiene como que la lentejuela" (has sequins) and expressing affection for the "super lindo" (super cute) unicorn pin. The background consistently displays a cluttered crafting table with various scrapbooking supplies under bright, even lighting.

Make your own journal.description: A pink journal cover with gold foil text 'LIFE IS MADE OF SMALL MOMENTS LIKE THIS!' and gold polka dots, held and rotated by a hand to show front and back, then placed on the table.

Gold Glitter Glue.description: A small bottle with a red applicator tip, containing gold glitter glue, held up to the camera. The speaker refers to this, along with other adhesive elements, as 'this for gluing, obviously, the embellishments,' indicating its purpose in the crafting process.

Clear Plastic Sheet with Adhesive Strips.description: A transparent sheet with white adhesive strips, picked up and held briefly by the hand before being set aside.

Cardboard patterns.description: A large pack labeled 'Cardboard patterns' with 32 sheets and 4x8 designs, featuring colorful geometric and floral patterns. The packaging includes multilingual text such as 'Carton orné', 'Modelos en carton', 'Karton ozdobny ze wzorami', 'Vzory lepenky', and 'Vzory kartónov', and is held up to the camera to display the front label.

Rhinestones Mix Bag.description: A transparent plastic bag filled with a colorful assortment of rhinestones in various sizes, shapes, and iridescent colors, labeled 'Rhinestones mix 200 gram' with a barcode, 'Made in China' text, and a handwritten price of '2€'. The contents shimmer as the bag is rotated.

Sticker Sheets.description: Multiple sheets of colorful, die-cut stickers with tropical, spring, and botanical themes, including flamingos, pineapples, cacti, succulents, dreamcatchers, and phrases like 'Happy TROPICAL,' 'Happy Spring,' 'Congratulations!,' and 'For you.' The sheets are flipped and rotated to showcase front and back, with the reverse side being plain white with pre-punched holes.

PAPIER KNUTSEL SET.description: A craft paper set package containing 8x A4 double-sided pattern paper, 8x A5 double-sided pattern paper, 4x die-cut paper, 2x sticker sheets, ribbon, 4x feathers, and 8x paper tags, opened and flipped through to reveal its contents.

Feathers.description: Two small feather embellishments, one brown and one white, attached to clear plastic strips, held and displayed by the hand.

Patterned Paper Sheets.description: Several sheets of double-sided patterned paper with designs including green leafy vines with black dots, a light blue gradient with floating feathers, a black-and-white polka dot pattern, a blue and white chevron with cacti and flowers, and a pink background with dreamcatchers and hummingbirds, flipped through to show both sides.

3D DIE-CUT IMAGES.description: Multiple packs labeled '3D DIE-CUT IMAGES' with multilingual text, containing layered, textured stickers. The female speaker describes them as suitable 'for sticking them in layers,' highlights themes like 'birthdays,' 'thanks and celebrate,' and praises their aesthetic as 'super pretty.' She also states their price as €1.90 per block/pack. The segment specifically shows one sheet with pastel-toned floral and inspirational designs, and another with retro-themed illustrations including children, vehicles, and various vintage signs.

EMBOSSING folder WITH CLEARSTAMPS.description: A transparent embossing folder with a grid pattern, labeled 'EMBOSSING folder WITH CLEARSTAMPS,' featuring clear stamps with designs such as a butterfly, a framed floral motif, and the phrase 'better together.' The folder is rotated and pointed to by a hand to highlight its features.

Suscríbete al Canal!.description: A text overlay in white font on a gradient pink-to-orange background, appearing visually in the lower center of the screen, encouraging viewers to subscribe as the female speaker concludes her video with a farewell message.

Follow me Text Screen.description: A black screen with white text displaying the social media handles and contact information for the creator, whose voice has just concluded the segment.

Action Bélgica.description: Text overlay indicating the retail store or location where the scrapbooking supplies were purchased, further confirmed by the female speaker sending "muchos saludos desde Bélgica" as she concludes the video.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a human hand, entering from the bottom left, manipulating a green package of 'emoji Sticky notes'. A female voice, speaking in Spanish, enthusiastically describes the playful designs, identifying one as a 'changuito' or 'mono' (monkey) with its tongue out, another as an 'apresurado' (hurried) emoji with a 'cara de angustia' (face of anguish), and a retro-style van as a 'combi' or 'Volkswagen' that she finds 'superlinda'. The hand rotates the package to display the 'emoji Sticky notes' label with multilingual text, then flips it to reveal the same designs and a '150X' quantity label on the back. The speaker confirms she bought 'estos tres post-its de de emojis' because she 'liked them a lot' and notes there are 'bastantes' of them. Subtle rustling sounds accompany the handling of the package. After placing the sticky notes down, the hand picks up a new item: a teal-colored kit labeled 'Make your own journal'. The speaker identifies this as 'este set' for making 'un álbum, un un journal, un diario', and mentions having previously bought a similar but 'diferente' journal. The kit features a pink journal cover with gold trim and the phrase 'LIFE IS MADE OF SMALL MOMENTS LIKE THIS!'. The hand holds the kit steady, pointing to the text and images on the packaging, which include illustrations of journaling supplies and a circular inset showing a hand placing stickers into a journal. The background consistently shows a cluttered crafting table filled with various scrapbooking supplies, including clear stamp sets, patterned paper packs, and embellishments, all arranged on a green cutting mat under bright, even lighting, suggesting a product showcase.

The video segment opens with a close-up of a hand holding a teal-colored 'Make your own journal' kit, which the speaker notes cost '2 euros con 34 centavos.' The hand rotates the kit to prominently display its front cover, featuring a pink journal with gold trim and the phrase 'LIFE IS MADE OF SMALL MOMENTS LIKE THIS!', before showing multilingual text on the side and back. The speaker mentions they haven't opened it yet but then states, 'Pero aquí lo abrimos' (But here we open it), aligning with the hand opening the kit to reveal two plain brown MDF journal covers with pre-drilled holes. The speaker confirms these are 'portadas... de MDF' (covers... of MDF). The hand separates the covers, showing their thickness and texture, while the speaker discusses their plan to decorate one of these journals, adding 'cosas' (things), and giving it away as part of a 'giveaway.' The hand then picks up a small bottle of gold glitter glue with a red applicator tip, which the speaker identifies as 'glitter dorado' (golden glitter) included in the kit, placing it on one of the covers. Finally, the hand picks up a clear plastic sheet with white adhesive strips, preparing to apply it to the journal cover. Throughout the segment, the background remains consistent with various scrapbooking supplies, including a 'Cardboard patterns' pack, an 'EMBOSSING folder WITH CLEARSTAMPS', and a container of rhinestones, all arranged on a green cutting mat, providing context for the 'embellecimientos' (embellishments) the speaker refers to. The lighting is bright and even, highlighting the textures and colors of the materials.

The segment opens with a close-up of a hand holding a pink journal cover from the 'Make your own journal' kit, featuring a white brushstroke design and gold polka dots. As the hand deliberately rotates the cover to reveal the gold phrase 'LIFE IS MADE OF SMALL MOMENTS LIKE THIS!', and then flips it to show the plain white back with two pre-drilled holes, the distinct sounds of rustling plastic and paper accompany the movements. A female voice, speaking in Spanish, introduces the item, stating, "It brings the white sheets that go here," and admits, "I'll show it to you, because I haven't seen it either. It seemed nice to me as I saw it in the box, but... but I haven't seen it." She then mentions, "It brings the rings to close it," followed by sounds of struggling to open a package, exclaiming, "Oh, it's like it's not coming out. I'm going to take this one out first." The hand then picks up a clear plastic sheet with adhesive strips and retrieves a gold glitter glue bottle, holding it up to the camera. The speaker confirms, "Okay. It brings this for gluing, obviously, the embellishments," and adds, "It brings this elastic, well, to close it. Now we have to see how I hold it." As the hand returns to showcase the journal cover's front design, emphasizing the gold text and polka dots, the speaker admires, "And well, the covers, these, seem very pretty to me. Seems very pretty." She contemplates its use, saying, "No, I don't know if I'm really going to use it for the cover or put it inside the page or use another paper. I don't know, I haven't decided," before concluding, "And that's the back part." Throughout, various scrapbooking supplies, including a 'Cardboard patterns' pack, an 'EMBOSSING folder WITH CLEARSTAMPS', and a container of rhinestones, are visible on a green cutting mat in the background.

The video opens with a close-up of hands holding vibrant, die-cut sticker sheets, featuring designs like hearts, film strips, speech bubbles, and inspirational phrases such as '#SMILE' and 'LIFE IS MADE OF SMALL MOMENTS LIKE THIS!'. A female voice, speaking Spanish, immediately describes these as "embellishments that are super cute" and "super pretty," noting that "you just have to remove them, they are already pre-cut." As the hands carefully flip through the sheets, revealing pastel, metallic, gold foil, and glittery elements, the speaker points out "this other one" and describes a specific design as "like a pocket," finding it "very pretty." The background shows a cluttered crafting workspace with items like a 'Cardboard patterns' pack and rhinestones on a green cutting mat. The hands continue to display the stickers, with the speaker mentioning designs "with banners" and reiterating that "all these are also like banners," and that "all of them are very pretty." The sheets are then flipped to reveal their plain white reverse side with pre-punched holes, which the speaker identifies as "the interior pages" and describes as "like cardstock, it's quite thick," emphasizing its substantial feel. The segment concludes with the hands holding a clear plastic sheet with adhesive strips and briefly showing a brown MDF journal cover with pre-drilled holes, suggesting the materials are part of a 'Make your own journal' kit, which the speaker intends to "decorate." Throughout, a gentle, upbeat background music plays, accompanied by subtle rustling sounds as the hands manipulate the crafting materials.

The segment opens with a close-up of a hand holding and rotating a pink journal cover from the 'Make your own journal' kit, which features the phrase 'LIFE IS MADE OF SMALL MOMENTS LIKE THIS!' in gold foil lettering and gold polka dots, before placing it down on a cluttered crafting table. A female voice, speaking in Spanish, comments, "But I think the one that turns out prettiest, I'll give it away," and then explains, "And well, stay tuned, maybe I'll post it on Instagram or here on YouTube, but it's going to be something for YouTube, so yes it will be, well, I'll post it on both places. I'll also leave it here and on Instagram." The hand then picks up a clear plastic sheet with adhesive strips, briefly displaying it before setting it aside. The speaker continues, "And finally, I'll show you some papers I bought, they are embellishments." Next, the hand picks up a package labeled 'PAPIER KNUTSEL SET', and the speaker corrects herself, "Well no, first this set. This is a set of papers." As the hand flips through the package, revealing its contents—8x A4 double-sided pattern paper, 8x A5 double-sided pattern paper, 4x die-cut paper, 2x sticker sheets, ribbon, 4x feathers, and 8x paper tags—the speaker identifies it as a "papel craft set," adding, "Uh, I had bought others, here it says paper paper craft set. Uh, I had already bought two others and this one is a spring one. This one, I hadn't seen it." She further details, "This one costs 1.60, the set, well it brings all these things inside." The hand then opens the package to show the individual items, including floral and geometric patterned papers, die-cut shapes, and sticker sheets with designs like cacti, dreamcatchers, and the word 'Spring'. The speaker says, "Well, I'll open it here with you. This one, I haven't seen it." The hand continues to flip through the vibrant sticker sheets, showcasing their designs and textures, before closing the package and placing it back on the table. The speaker concludes by mentioning other sets she bought, "I bought another one that is a unicorn one, one that is botanical and..." Throughout the segment, light rustling sounds accompany the handling of materials, and the background consistently shows 'Scrapbooking Supplies' like a 'Cardboard patterns' pack, 'EMBOSSING folder WITH CLEARSTAMPS', and rhinestones on a green cutting mat, all under bright, even lighting.

The video opens with a close-up of a hand deliberately opening a 'PAPIER KNUTSEL SET' craft paper package. A female speaker, conversing in Spanish, notes that this is one of "four sets in total" and expresses surprise, stating she "hadn't seen this one before." As the hand flips through the included sticker sheets, revealing vibrant die-cut designs of flowers, dreamcatchers, cacti, toucans, and phrases like 'Happy Birthday,' 'Love,' 'XO,' and 'Let's party!', the speaker identifies them as "two sheets of stickers" and points out "birthday" themes and "cactus" designs, remarking that they are "very pretty." The distinct sound of paper flipping accompanies these actions. Next, the hand displays two small feather embellishments, one brown and one white, attached to clear plastic strips, which the speaker refers to as "little feathers." She also mentions a "white ribbon" as part of the set. The hand then continues to flip through several sheets of double-sided patterned paper, revealing designs including floral patterns, polka dots, geometric grids, and cacti, with one sheet featuring the phrase 'Do what makes you Happy,' and another pink sheet with pineapples and the word 'Love.' The speaker identifies these as "cardstock," noting their "thick and double-sided" quality, and points out a "cactus" design on one of the sheets. The background consistently shows a cluttered crafting table with various scrapbooking supplies, including a green cutting mat and other packages, all brightly lit, emphasizing the detailed contents of the set.

The video opens with a close-up of hands methodically flipping through a vibrant collection of sticker sheets and patterned paper on a cluttered crafting workspace, featuring a green cutting mat and various embellishments. A female voice immediately begins describing the designs in Spanish, pointing out a pink sheet with "piñas" (pineapples), then a white sheet with a large black-and-white "pluma" (feather), followed by "más plumas" (more feathers) and "florecitas" (little flowers) on a floral pattern. As a pink flamingo on a geometric background is displayed, the voice admires it as "qué bonito, un flamingo." Next, a green sheet with "colibrí" (hummingbird) and cactus designs is revealed. The speaker then highlights various die-cut "tags" that "tienen foil todas" (all have foil), noting one for "cumpleaños" (birthday) and another saying "solo para ti" (just for you), while also mentioning "más plumas" (more feathers) and "Suxo" (succulents) designs, calling one "bien bonito." The hands continue to display other "cartulinas en A4" (A4 cardstock) sheets, including a dense floral pattern, a grey background with pink polka dots, and one that says "amor es todo lo que necesitas" (love is all you need), which the speaker finds "qué bonito." As more sheets are flipped, including white ones with "piñas" (pineapples) and a textured beige sheet, the speaker points out "otra piña acá" (another pineapple here) and a sheet with "Suxo" (succulents) and "cactus," exclaiming "Ay, mira qué bonito con cactus!" The bright, even lighting consistently highlights the colors and details as the hands smoothly rotate and flip each sheet, emphasizing the variety and quality of the designs in what appears to be a creative product showcase.

The video segment opens with a close-up of a hand flipping through various patterned paper sheets, set to gentle, upbeat instrumental music. A female voice comments in Spanish, initially mentioning "un plan bien bonito" (a very nice plan) and the act of "sonreír" (to smile). As the hand displays a light blue gradient paper with floating feathers, the voice notes, "Y este tiene plumitas" (And this one has little feathers), calling it "qué bonito" (how pretty) and "super lindo." Other papers shown include green leafy vines with black dots, a black-and-white polka dot, and a blue and white chevron with cacti and flowers. When a pink sheet with dreamcatchers and hummingbirds is revealed, the speaker points out "un atrapasueños y un colibrí" (a dreamcatcher and a hummingbird). The hand then transitions to vibrant sticker sheets, which the speaker identifies as "die-cuts" that need to be removed. She highlights specific elements like "cactus," "plantitas de cactus" (little cactus plants), "mariposa" (butterfly), and "una guirnalda de flores de cactus" (a garland of cactus flowers). She also points out a hummingbird and a dreamcatcher, and surprisingly, an "un buh" (an owl) among the die-cut stickers, which also feature tropical themes like flamingos, pineapples, and phrases such as 'Dream,' 'Happy Birthday,' 'Congrats!,' 'Spring,' and 'TROPICAL.' Throughout, the background consistently shows a green cutting mat and scattered crafting supplies, including a container of rhinestones and a 'Cardboard patterns' box, reinforcing the scrapbooking context under bright, even lighting.

The segment opens with a close-up view of a person's hands deliberately flipping through multiple sheets of colorful, die-cut stickers, set against a crafting workspace with various scrapbooking supplies on a green cutting mat, all accompanied by light, upbeat background music. The speaker immediately begins describing the first sheet, noting "flamingos" and "feathers," though expressing slight uncertainty about the latter, before confirming the "tropical" theme with a "toucan." As the hand rotates the sheet, the speaker points out a "dreamcatcher," "cacti," a "garland," and a "pineapple." Moving to the second sheet, the speaker exclaims "Oh, how pretty," and reads text like "felicidades" (congratulations), identifying "flowers," "cactus," and another "dreamcatcher" and "garland," specifically mentioning a sticker that says "primavera" (spring). The third sheet is introduced with the speaker reading "para ti" (for you) and "amor" (love), further detailing "feathers," a "wooden box" design, a "banner," and various "little plants and cactus flowers," along with more "cacti" and "dreamcatchers." Finally, the speaker confirms this is the "last page," showing the plain white backing with pre-punched holes, and concludes positively, stating "It's super good."

The video opens with a close-up of a human hand, belonging to a female Spanish speaker, deliberately flipping through a stack of colorful sticker sheets, revealing various designs including dreamcatchers, feathers, and floral patterns. The speaker expresses her delight, stating that she liked a previous purchase so much that she's now buying these, exclaiming, "I'm taking it! It's super pretty!" The hand then moves to a collection of packs, which the speaker identifies as "libros de die-cut" (die-cut books/packs), prominently labeled '3D DIE-CUT IMAGES' with multilingual text indicating 12 sheets and inclusion of labels and embellishments. As the hand flips through several of these packs, accompanied by faint rustling sounds, the speaker counts them, "one, two, three, four," and notes that while there were more options, these were her favorites. She showcases vibrant themes such as flowers, butterflies, cakes, and tea cups, with phrases like 'Get well soon,' 'Hello Sunshine,' 'Cheers,' and 'You're my cup of tea!' The speaker explains that all the sheets are die-cut, meant to be separated and used as layered adornments, mentioning "flowers and everything." The stickers are layered and textured, featuring die-cut shapes with added embellishments like buttons and ribbons. The hand rotates and tilts the sheets to display the details, emphasizing the variety and quality of the 3D elements, with the speaker noting that they can be glued in layers and identifying some as "for birthdays." The background remains consistent with a green cutting mat and scattered crafting supplies, including rhinestones and other embellishments, reinforcing the scrapbooking context.

As gentle, upbeat instrumental music plays, a human hand, entering from the bottom right, deliberately and gently flips through a series of vibrant 3D die-cut image packs. A male voice, speaking in Spanish, explains that these are used as "3D embellishments" (embellecimiento en 3D) and can be layered or used separately. The hand carefully rotates and tilts each page to display the front and back of the intricately layered and textured stickers, highlighting detailed designs like pink and yellow flowers, black-and-white birds, and decorative banners, emphasizing their layered construction and embellishments such as buttons and glitter. The voice reads aloud various phrases from the stickers, including "felicidades" (congratulations), "buena suerte" (good luck), "disfruta el viaje" (enjoy the trip), "feliz cumpleaños" (happy birthday), "por una casa nueva" (for a new house), "mejórate pronto" (get well soon), "disfruta" (enjoy), "para ti" (for you), and "disfruta el momento" (enjoy the moment). He also identifies themes like bicycles, butterflies, and what he thinks might be lavender or small plants and cacti, commenting on how "very pretty" they are and that they are for "decorating little cards" (adornar tarjetitas). The bright, even lighting allows clear visibility of the stickers, while the background reveals a crafting workspace with a green cutting mat and containers of rhinestones, reinforcing the papercrafting context. The speaker concludes by remarking how "super cute" the stickers are.

The segment opens with upbeat, light instrumental music as a hand, entering from the bottom right, continues to showcase 3D die-cut image packs. The hand flips through multiple packs, labeled '3D DIE-CUT IMAGES' with multilingual text, rotating each to display intricate, layered stickers. The speaker points out "pajaritos" (little birds), "flamingos," and a "colibrí" (hummingbird) on the first sheet, exclaiming "qué bonito estos colibrís y los flamingos están super lindos" (how beautiful these hummingbirds and the flamingos are super cute). As the hand moves to other sheets, the speaker describes the images as "vintage," noting that "las imágenes y los dibujitos son son vintage" (the images and drawings are vintage), and expresses a personal preference, "a mí a veces me da, me gusta lo vintage," explaining that their choice "depende del proyecto que esté haciendo" (depends on the project I'm doing), whether it's "más nuevo" (newer) or "vintage o shabby." They find them "super lindo, todo, todos me gustaron" (super cute, everything, I liked them all), likening some to "dibujitos de los 50" (drawings from the 50s). The hand highlights a sheet with school-themed stickers, then vintage-style illustrations, which the speaker points out as "uno de niña" (one of a girl), "uno de sonríe" (one that says 'smile'), and "unos niñitos" (some little children). The final sheets include pirate themes and playful children's illustrations, with the speaker noting "una chica en la en el salón de clases y estos acá jugando" (a girl in the classroom and these ones here playing). Throughout, the background maintains the scrapbooking context with a green cutting mat and rhinestones, under bright, even lighting.

The segment opens with a close-up of a hand, accompanied by light, upbeat background music, deliberately showcasing a sheet of 3D die-cut images featuring vintage school-themed children, floral elements, and text like 'Smile.' A female speaker remarks in Spanish, "No sé, está super bonito" (I don't know, it's super pretty), as the hand rotates the sheet to reveal a girl with a book and a child, which the speaker describes as "comiendo nieve o algo así" (eating snow or something like that). The scene then transitions to a second pack of 3D die-cut images, this time with a retro, vintage aesthetic, displaying labels and signs for 'Garage,' 'Gas,' 'BBQ Party,' and 'Beer.' The hand flips through this sheet, pointing to specific stickers like 'Free Beer Tomorrow' and 'Bouling Zone,' emphasizing their detailed, textured, and layered nature. The speaker expresses her enthusiasm for this second sheet, stating, "Me compré este porque también son vintage, pero igual y un poco varonil, eh, y no sé, a mí me pareció muy lindo" (I bought this one because they are also vintage, but maybe a bit masculine, huh, and I don't know, I found it very cute). She continues, "Toda esta página me gustó, me gustó mucho... ay, me lo voy a llevar" (I liked this whole page, I liked it a lot... oh, I'm going to take it), and further describes the stickers as "propaganda vieja, propaganda de gasolina, de cerveza, de de aceite de motor" (old propaganda, gasoline propaganda, beer, motor oil propaganda). Throughout, the background remains a crafting workspace with a green cutting mat and rhinestones, reinforcing the creative context.

A hand continues to deliberately flip through and rotate various 3D die-cut image sheets, showcasing their intricate details and layered textures, all set against a crafting workspace with a green cutting mat and rhinestones.
